title: Inventory survey, CRVS, and health-touchpoint data sources for birth registration analysis
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates survey-based registration measures, administrative completeness sources, proof-of-registration measures, health-touchpoint data, and population denominators into usable, limited, and rejected categories.
inputs: UNICEF birth registration database and methodology notes
DHS birth-registration indicator documentation or microdata access notes
MICS birth-registration module or survey-coverage notes
World Bank SDG 16.9.1 metadata and indicator access
WHO and WHO-UNICEF CRVS and health-sector guidance
country-specific CRVS or health-registry documentation where available
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each source for URL, date, measure family, access conditions, grain, and limitations, especially whether it measures under-5 survey status, documentary proof, or administrative live-birth completeness.
done_condition: At least six candidate sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering measure family, timing, geographic grain, and health-linkage relevance.

title: Write a reconciliation note for survey registration, certificate possession, and CRVS completeness
outcome: A short analytic note that states which birth-registration measure answers which question, why the measures diverge, and which comparisons are prohibited without qualification.
expected_artifact: validation.md and outputs.md updates with measure-family rules.
validation_method: Reviewer checks that each measure is tied to its exact numerator, denominator, time reference, and known mismatch against other measures.
done_condition: No comparison is left ambiguous about whether it refers to under-5 survey status, certificate possession, or administrative birth-registration completeness.

title: Select one to three countries where sub-national birth-registration work is genuinely feasible
outcome: A country-selection memo that names which countries have adequate survey recency, sub-national identifiers, denominator sources, and at least one usable health-touchpoint or administrative comparison path.
expected_artifact: validation.md update with inclusion and exclusion rules.
validation_method: Replicator checks that selected countries meet the documented minimum data requirements and that rejected countries have explicit reasons.
done_condition: Every selected country has documented measure family, survey year, sub-national grain, denominator source, and health-linkage feasibility note.

title: Test whether strong health-system reach coexists with weak birth registration
outcome: A bounded analysis asking whether one selected country shows a plausible registration gap despite stronger facility-birth or immunization contact, framed as a missed-opportunity test rather than a causal proof.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and validation.md updates with a country-specific analytic wedge.
validation_method: Replicator reruns the comparison and confirms the touchpoint metric, registration metric, denominator choice, and uncertainty statements.
done_condition: The artifact states the exact touchpoint, the exact birth-registration measure, what the gap does and does not imply, and why the result is not yet an intervention recommendation.

title: Red-team coercive use, denominator abuse, and false comparability
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how birth-registration gap outputs could justify punitive enforcement, hide survey-vs-CRVS mismatch, or mislabel administrative weakness as caregiver non-compliance.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Mitigations are confirmed or publication is blocked, then a second reviewer replicates the check that each high-risk comparison is either removed or explicitly qualified.
done_condition: Every field-facing claim is either blocked, softened to analytic language, or gated behind explicit warnings about coercive use, denominator mismatch, and measure-family confusion, with replicated red-team review before publication.

title: Check whether outputs match registrar, immunization, or child-health decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the artifact would change a real decision for a civil registrar, immunization manager, or health-information officer without generating worse exclusion incentives.
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with named use and non-use cases.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the decision, user, timing, required workflow, and misuse risk, then a second reviewer replicates the check on the stated non-use cases.
done_condition: The artifact has a named user, named decision, named service touchpoint, explicit non-use cases, and replicated field-reality review before publication.
